stories.title: Deadlock over Iran's nuclear program and the Strait of Hormuz cripples peace efforts
stories.description: Two months after the U.S.-Israeli attack on Iran started the war, peace talks are on hold, with control of the Strait of Hormuz and the future of Iran's nuclear program as the two main points of contention.

stories.title: Iran's flurry of diplomacy continues in Russia, as Trump reviews Iran's latest proposal
stories.description: Iran's foreign minister arrived in Russia on Monday, after a whirlwind weekend of diplomacy, seeking to gain political leverage and foreign backing as peace talks with the U.S. remain on hold.

stories.title: Russian superyacht sails through Strait of Hormuz despite blockade
stories.description: The 141m-long vessel, linked to a close ally of Russian President Vladimir Putin, cleared the waterway despite an ongoing blockade.

stories.title: Israeli strikes kill 14 in Lebanon amid ongoing ceasefire
stories.description: The US announced three days ago that the ceasefire was extended by three weeks after talks.

stories.title: Trump cancels US envoys' trip to Pakistan for talks on Iran war
stories.description: Iran had earlier said there were no plans for a direct meeting with a US delegation led by Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner.

stories.title: Netanyahu orders army to 'vigorously attack' Hezbollah in Lebanon
stories.description: The Israeli PM's directive comes as six people were killed by strikes in Lebanon despite a ceasefire being extended by three weeks.

stories.title: Who is making decisions in Iran?
stories.description: Iran's new supreme leader is supposed to have the final say, but in practice the picture is far murkier.

stories.title: Trump says Israel-Lebanon ceasefire to be extended by three weeks
stories.description: Envoys from the two countries met in Washington as the fragile truce was due to expire on Sunday.

stories.title: US boards ship carrying Iran oil as Trump threatens mine-laying vessels
stories.description: Central Command said it has intercepted 33 vessels since imposing a blockade on ships coming to and from Iran.

stories.title: Couple discovers Lebanon home destroyed by Israel from satellite image
stories.description: Joe Elias contacted BBC Verify following reports that his village was among several destroyed by Israeli forces.

stories.title: What we know about the Israel-Lebanon ceasefire
stories.description: A 10-day truce between the two countries has been extended by three weeks after talks in Washington.

stories.title: In Tehran, money is short and a return to war looms over daily life
stories.description: The BBC's Lyse Doucet reports from Iran, where the buzz of busy shopping streets masks deep uncertainty over the country's future.

stories.title: Charles on a mission: Can king's state visit salvage US-UK ties?
stories.description: Call it the royal trump card: King Charles dispatched to Washington on a state visit at the lowest point in the Special Relationship in at least seven decades. We'll ask if catering to Donald Trump's enthusiasm for the British crown can dispel the bad blood over tariffs, the war ...

stories.title: King Charles to promote British-American unity in speech to Congress
stories.description: Britain's King Charles will press the importance of unity with the United States and the need to defend democratic values in an address to Congress on Tuesday at a time of deep divisions between the two countries over the war in Iran. Charles ​and Queen Camilla ‌are on a four-day...

stories.title: Hezbollah-Israel ceasefire under strain as both sides trade fire
stories.description: Israel's military on Tuesday warned residents of more than a dozen villages and towns in southern Lebanon to immediately evacuate and head northwards, saying Hezbollah's "violation of the ceasefire" was compelling it to act. FRANCE 24's Noga Tarnopolsky reports from Jerusalem.

stories.title: UAE to quit global oil cartel OPEC, citing 'national interests'
stories.description: The United Arab Emirates announced on Tuesday it will quit the global oil cartel OPEC and OPEC+ to focus on "national interests" as energy prices continue to climb amid the ongoing Iran conflict.

stories.title: Trump approval dips to record low amid Iran war, inflation woes: Poll
stories.description: Only 22 percent of US voters back the president's performance on the cost of living, Reuters/Ipsos survey suggests.

stories.title: Is a US-Iran deal still possible?
stories.description: As the Strait of Hormuz stays largely shut and Washington sends mixed signals, talks run through Islamabad and Moscow.

stories.title: UAE leaves OPEC in blow to oil cartel amid war on Iran
stories.description: The United ‌Arab Emirates has announced it’s withdrawing from OPEC and OPEC+.

stories.title: Gulf leaders meet in Saudi Arabia for first time since start of war on Iran
stories.description: High-level GCC summit in Jeddah embodies 'unified Gulf stance' towards the war, Qatar's emir says.

stories.title: Qatar says using Hormuz Strait as political weapon is ‘unacceptable’
stories.description: Qatar’s foreign ministry spokesman Majed al-Ansari said that the Strait of Hormuz must not be used as a bargaining chip.

stories.title: The cost of 76 years of US wars, from Korea to Iran
stories.description: Al Jazeera breaks down the human and financial cost of decades of US-led wars and its latest war on Iran.

stories.title: What’s in Iran’s latest proposal – and how has the US responded?
stories.description: Trump administration signals rejection of Iran’s offer, citing incomplete resolution of Tehran’s nuclear ambitions.

stories.title: Kandice in southern Lebanon
stories.description: A video diary for Al Jazeera

stories.title: 5 sentenced to life in Bahrain for plotting 'terrorist acts' with Iran
stories.description: Five people were sentenced to life in Bahrain on Tuesday for plotting “terrorist and hostile acts” with Iran, which bombarded the tiny Gulf state during the Middle East war. Two Afghans and three Bahrainis were convicted and a fourth Bahraini was acquitted by the High Criminal Co...

stories.title: Britain's King Charles meets Trump in bid to salvage ties
stories.description: Britain’s King Charles III met Donald Trump at the White House early Tuesday, kicking off a high-stakes state visit shadowed by transatlantic tensions and a new alleged attempt to assassinate the US president. Behind the warm welcome for Charles and Queen Camilla in front of the ...

stories.title: Russia backs Iran as peace efforts with US remain stalled
stories.description: • Putin says Moscow will do everything to secure peace deal• Araghchi blames ‘excessive US demands’ for failure of dialogue• White House stays mum as Trump meets security aides to discuss Tehran’s proposal• Rubio says Iran ‘serious’ about deal, cites bleak economic outlook• Iran,...

stories.title: Israel strikes Bekaa Valley in violation of ceasefire
stories.description: BEIRUT: The Israeli military began carrying out strikes in eastern Lebanon on Monday, expanding the scope of its bombing campaign during a ceasefire that has failed to fully halt hostilities with Hezbollah. The strikes on Lebanon’s eastern Bekaa Valley marked the first time the a...

stories.title: Muted traffic in Hormuz strains Gulf economies
stories.description: • Seven ships pass vital waterway in a day, compared to pre-war average of 140• Iranian lawmaker says military to manage maritime route as UN chief urges toll-free waterway• UAE rebukes Gulf allies for ‘meek’ political and military response to Tehran strikes• Germany’s Merz says ...

stories.title: Bahrain revokes citizenship of 69 people for 'glorifying or sympathising' with Iranian attacks
stories.description: Bahrain revoked the citizenship of 69 people over what it described as sympathy with Iran’s hostile acts and collaboration with foreign entities, the kingdom’s interior ministry said in a statement. The ministry said that the 69 people included accused individuals and their famil...

stories.title: Israel-Iran war LIVE: Trump claims Iran 'just' informed U.S. it's in 'state of collapse'
stories.description: Ceasefire efforts appears at a standstill, with Iran saying Washington could no longer dictate terms

stories.title: ​Battle of wills: On the U.S. war on Iran
stories.description: The U.S. should lift its blockade so that Iran can open the Strait of Hormuz

stories.title: Iraqi president nominates Ali al-Zaidi as PM-designate
stories.description: In January, Mr. Trump threatened to stop all support for Iraq if two-time ex-premier Maliki, who has close ties to Iran, returned to power.

stories.title: Trump hails British as ‘friends’ as King Charles III visits amid Iran tensions
stories.description: The President has spoken in glowing terms about King Charles, repeatedly referring to the monarch as his “friend” and a “great guy”

stories.title: UAE leaves OPEC and OPEC+ in huge blow to global oil producers' group
stories.description: The move came after the UAE, a regional business hub and one of Washington’s most important allies, criticised fellow Arab states for not doing enough to protect it from numerous Iranian attacks during the war

stories.title: U.S. says examining latest Iran proposal on Hormuz
stories.description: Iran has blockaded the waterway — a vital conduit for oil and gas shipments — since the start of the U.S.-Israeli offensive two months ago, sending shockwaves through the global economy

stories.title: Sanctions against Russian businessmen affect even distant relatives — Putin
stories.description: The Russian economy is operating in the environment of serious external challenges for many years, including the difficult economic situation, illegitimate sanctions and certain other adverse circumstances, the Russian leader noted

stories.title: Expert cites three factors behind UAE decision to exit OPEC, OPEC+
stories.description: Dmitry Kasatkin emphasized that Iran's attacks and the Hormuz blockade had demonstrated that getting Tehran to protect everyone's interests in OPEC would be impossible

stories.title: Iran does not consider war with US, Israel to be over — army
stories.description: Islamic Republic Army Spokesman Mohammad Akraminiya noted that the conditions are still considered wartime

stories.title: Iran continued to produce UAVs even during war with US, Israel — army
stories.description: According to army representative Mohammad Akraminia, if the United States and Israel commit aggression again and endanger Iran's security, they will definitely face a tougher response than before
